title: Photo Spots
description: The first version is style-led: use it to build a fast location shortlist before your trip.
recommendTitle: Start with these three spot types
recommendDescription: These first-pass spot ideas follow the visual rhythm of {{city}}.

notes.alley: Best when the lane is still quiet
notes.square: Edges are easier to control than the center
notes.breakwater: Long lens compression works well here
notes.cliff: Clean weather keeps the frame minimal
notes.lookout: Mist and layered ridges add depth
notes.cabin: Warm lights against cool air make strong contrast

angles.leadingLines: Use lines in the lane to pull the subject into the center.
angles.framePeople: Keep the subject off-center so the place still tells the story.
angles.useLayering: Leave room for foreground, middle ground, and distance.
angles.reflectiveWater: Use water reflections to complete the frame.
angles.longLens: Compress the sea and subject with a longer lens.
angles.negativeSpace: Give the sky or sea more breathing room.
angles.depthFog: Let fog and ridge lines build depth.
angles.pathStory: Use the path as the narrative spine of the frame.
angles.warmContrast: Play warm light sources against cool surroundings.
